title: Petit Trees (sketch)
alt: 'Petit' being a reference to Le Petit Prince, which I only thought about halfway through the sketch
transcript:
[Some little rectangles are drawn to the left of the scan.]
[Two trees are growing on opposite sides of a very small sphere. Their roots cover most of the planet's surface.]
explanation: This comic does not present a particular point, it's just a picture drawn by Randall.  (in French Le Petit Prince) is a novella written by  in 1943, about the titular Little Prince, who lives on a very small nearly-spherical asteroid and visits other inhabited asteroids and eventually the Earth. The book is filled with drawings of the asteroid, the prince, and the travels they make. It is noted how, on occasion,  can begin to grow on these asteroids, and should they not be immediately uprooted, the growth of their roots would tear the asteroid apart. This illustration appears to be just before the trees tear apart the asteroid, based on how far the roots have spread.The Little Prince would later be referenced in the what if? article , in 618: Asteroid, and in 1350: Lorenz at the end of the space trip branch.
